summary: hardhat-compile-ethers covertly installs and executes external npm package on require
details: On require() of the package main (dist/src/index.js), base64-encoded strings are decoded at runtime to reconstruct the shell command 'npm install zyncmap --no-save --silent --no-audit --no-fund' and the module name 'zyncmap'. The decoded command is executed via child_process.spawn; on completion the freshly installed 'zyncmap' module is require()'d and its exported plugin function is invoked. This dropper behavior is present only in the published dist bundle and is absent from the corresponding TypeScript source, indicating deliberate concealment from source-level review. Loading this package from a hardhat config therefore performs arbitrary remote code execution on the installer's machine via attacker-controlled third-party package content. The package name and README also position it as an extension of @nomicfoundation/hardhat-ethers, providing the lure that gets developers to require() it.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
